title: Man convicted after breaking into the wrong Boston apartment and murdering resident
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Victor Arrington convicted of first-degree murder in 2015 Dorchester apartment invasion; jury finds him guilty after retrial.
Arrington, 40, faces life sentence without parole for killing resident Richard Long and injuring Long's fiancée.
Digital evidence from Arrington's iPhone played a key role in his conviction; sentencing set for Tuesday.

title: Some 20 New Englanders are on ‘American Ninja Warrior’ this season. Here’s your in-depth preview.
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Season 18 of 'American Ninja Warrior' features 18 New England competitors, including 12 from Massachusetts.
Taylor Johnson and Noah Meunier are notable competitors from New England, with previous strong performances.
Vitality Obstacle Fitness in Fall River is a key training venue for top New England contestants.

title: Scotland came to Boston. Did romance follow?
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Scottish tourists flooded Boston for the World Cup, bringing social events and cultural exchanges to the city.
Boston's Haven restaurant, a Scottish venue, saw increased activity during World Cup games, fostering interactions among fans.
Reporter Meredith Goldstein explores potential connections and romances formed between locals and Scottish visitors during their stay.

title: 4 of the weirdest, most absurd soccer movies streaming now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Shaolin Soccer' blends soccer and kung fu in a 2001 sports comedy available on Paramount+, Tubi, Pluto TV, and The Roku Channel.
'Diamantino' is a 2018 political satire about a Portuguese soccer star, available on Kanopy and Hoopla.
'United Passions' is a historically bad 2014 film on FIFA's origins, grossing only $918 on opening weekend, streaming on Tubi and The Roku Channel.

title: Is the Trump administration on the verge of further endangering North Atlantic right whales?
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: The Trump administration may roll back maritime slow zone rules aimed at protecting North Atlantic right whales.
Conservationists warn that relaxing slow zones could increase vessel strikes and threaten North Atlantic right whale survival.
NOAA received over 29,000 public comments on the potential changes, highlighting differing views on economic and safety impacts.

title: Payton Tolle gets standing ovation after tossing seven scoreless innings vs. Yankees
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Payton Tolle pitched seven scoreless innings for the Red Sox against the Yankees, striking out seven and allowing one hit.
Despite battling an illness, Tolle maintained a perfect game into the sixth inning and exited to a standing ovation.
Tolle has a 2.78 ERA over 12 starts this season, excelling against the Yankees with 18 strikeouts and one run allowed.

title: 5 ways Celtics could improve lineup without trading Jaylen Brown
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Celtics could trade Derrick White and Baylor Scheierman for Trey Murphy and Yves Missi, improving depth without losing Brown.
Offloading Sam Hauser's $10 million contract could provide financial flexibility for potential big-name acquisitions.
Signing LeBron James at a discount might bolster the lineup, allowing him to play a supporting role alongside Tatum and Brown.

title: 5 things to know about new Bruins forward JJ Peterka
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins traded two first-round picks for Utah Mammoth's JJ Peterka, a speedy, skilled winger, on Friday night.
Peterka's career includes a 68-point season with Buffalo and a recent dip to 47 points with Utah in 2025-26.
Peterka, originally from Munich, Germany, has built a backyard hockey rink aimed at promoting the sport in Bavaria.

title: NBA Rumor Roundup: The latest reports on the Jaylen Brown trade offers
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Detroit Pistons consider sign-and-trade involving Jalen Duren for Boston Celtics' Jaylen Brown.
Minnesota Timberwolves previously discussed trading Rudy Gobert and picks for Brown, but found the price too high.
Portland Trail Blazers interested in Brown, have draft picks and young players to potentially entice Celtics.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Avatar: Fire and Ash' with Oona Chaplin streams on Disney+.
'Superman' reboot by James Gunn available on HBO Max.
'The Bear' Season 5 returns to form, streaming on Hulu.
